When you select a level, all modifiers at that level and below apply.0No AscensionPlay without any Ascension modifiers.1Swarming ElitesElites spawn more often.2Weary TravelerAncients only heal 80% of your missing HP.3PovertyEnemies and Treasure Chests drop 25% less Gold.4Tight BeltStart each run with 1 less potion slot.5Ascender's BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Ironclad returns in Slay

metadata.title: The Ironclad – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Ironclad returns in Slay the Spire 2 as the steadfast master of martial prowess and demonic pacts. While retaining his identity as a heavy hitter who manipulates HP and the Exhaust pile, his kit in Slay the Spire 2 introduces more nuanced synergies between Vulnerability, Self-Damage, and Exhaust interactions, making his tactical ceiling higher than ever.Evolution of Core Mechanics in Slay the Spire 2Enhanced Vulnerability SynergiesIn Slay the Spire 2, the Ironclad's ability to exploit Vulnerability has expanded from a simple damage multiplier into a central mechanical pillar.Tremble allows for 0-cost application of Vulnerability, while Bully scales directly with the number of turns of Vulnerability remaining on an enemy.Powers like Cruelty turn Vulnerable into a real payoff by making those enemies take additional

metadata.title: The Ironclad – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: while Bully scales directly with the number of turns of Vulnerability remaining on an enemy.Powers like Cruelty turn Vulnerable into a real payoff by making those enemies take additional damage, sharpening the class's burst windows.Self-Damage & High-Risk ScalingThe "Self-Damage" archetype is more prominent in Slay the Spire 2. The Ironclad now possesses more versatile ways to trade health for immediate power.Blood Wall converts HP into Block, while Breakthrough provides AOE damage at a vital cost. Hemokinesis has been slightly tuned down in base damage to compensate for the improved self-damage synergy cards.This "blood price" package is rewarded by cards like Spite, which turn losing HP this turn into immediate extra hits and help self-damage turns keep tempo.Advanced Exhaust & Graveyard InteractionExhausting cards remains a cornerstone of the Ironclad's strategy in Slay the Spire 2, but with new "conditional play" layers.A standout new mechanic involves cards interacting with the

metadata.title: The Ironclad – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: InteractionExhausting cards remains a cornerstone of the Ironclad's strategy in Slay the Spire 2, but with new "conditional play" layers.A standout new mechanic involves cards interacting with the Exhaust Pile in real-time. The rare attack Pact's End turns your Exhaust Pile into a concrete threshold, only unlocking once you have at least 3 cards there.Cards like Ashen Strike scale directly with the size of your Exhaust Pile, while Howl from Beyond can fire itself from that pile, adding a genuinely recursive layer to graveyard management.Strategic ArchetypesThe Ironclad's returning kit in Slay the Spire 2 supports several distinct build paths, each enhanced with new mechanics:🛡️ The Juggernaut (Plating & Armor)The Plating mechanic through Stone Armor offers lasting defensive layers, encouraging a tank plan that does not need to rebuild Block from scratch every turn.⚔️ The Master of StrikesWith Hellraiser, basic "Strike" cards become automatic triggers — played whenever they are drawn.

metadata.title: The Ironclad – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: a tank plan that does not need to rebuild Block from scratch every turn.⚔️ The Master of StrikesWith Hellraiser, basic "Strike" cards become automatic triggers — played whenever they are drawn. This breathes new life into often-discarded basic cards and supports a "Strike-heavy" deck archetype.🔄 The Aggressive RecyclerAggression ensures your discard pile is never stagnant, automatically returning and upgrading a random attack to your hand each turn — relentless pressure and permanent deck scaling mid-combat.🔥 Exhaust RecursionUse Ashen Strike to turn Exhaust size into raw damage, unlock Pact's End once you cross the 3-card threshold, then let Howl from Beyond keep firing from the Exhaust Pile.💀 Vulnerability ExecutionerChain Tremble into Bully with Cruelty for devastating Vulnerability-scaling combos that turn debuffs into lethal damage windows.Notable Changes from Slay the Spire 1Several classic Ironclad cards have undergone balance shifts for Slay the Spire 2's

metadata.title: The Ironclad – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: Vulnerability-scaling combos that turn debuffs into lethal damage windows.Notable Changes from Slay the Spire 1Several classic Ironclad cards have undergone balance shifts for Slay the Spire 2's meta:CHANGEDBludgeon: Now Uncommon (previously Rare), making high-burst damage more accessible in early acts.CHANGEDDark Embrace: Returns to Rare status to balance out the increased frequency of exhaust synergies in the sequel.CHANGEDHemokinesis: Slightly tuned down in base damage to compensate for the improved self-damage synergy cards in STS2.Explore MoreThe Silent — Sly, Shivs & PoisonNecrobinder — Doom, Souls & OstyThe Regent — Stars, Forge & MinionsDetailsNo additional details availableComments (0)Be the first to comment.Sign in to join the discussionSign inSlaytheSpire2.ggSlay the Spire 2 database, tools, and community links.© 2026 SlaytheSpire2.ggPage NavigationCharactersCardsRelicsPotionsEnemiesEncountersEventsAncientsTimelineGuidesTier List MakerLegal & SitePrivacy PolicyTerms of

When you select a level, all modifiers at that level and below apply.0No AscensionPlay without any Ascension modifiers.1Swarming ElitesElites spawn more often.2Weary TravelerAncients only heal 80% of your missing HP.3PovertyEnemies and Treasure Chests drop 25% less Gold.4Tight BeltStart each run with 1 less potion slot.5Ascender's BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Silent returns in Slay the Spire 2, maintaining her

metadata.title: The Silent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Silent returns in Slay the Spire 2, maintaining her identity as a deadly acrobat who specializes in a flurry of small attacks and debilitating toxins. While veterans will find her core playstyle familiar, Slay the Spire 2 introduces a revolutionary keyword — Sly — that fundamentally transforms how her discard-heavy deck operates, making her more explosive and versatile than ever.Core New Mechanic: SlyDynamic Utility: The most significant addition to the Silent's arsenal in Slay the Spire 2. Cards with the Sly keyword gain a powerful dual-use property: they can be played traditionally for their Energy cost, or discarded to trigger their effect automatically for 0 Energy.Engine Optimization: This mechanic turns discard effects from a "cost" into a "trigger," allowing the Silent to play high-cost cards even when Energy is low.Master Planning: The

metadata.title: The Silent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: for 0 Energy.Engine Optimization: This mechanic turns discard effects from a "cost" into a "trigger," allowing the Silent to play high-cost cards even when Energy is low.Master Planning: The rare power Master Planner expands Sly access across more of your Skill package, while Hand Trick can temporarily wire a single key Skill into that same engine.Evolution of Shiv SynergiesThe iconic Shiv playstyle receives a massive power-up in Slay the Spire 2, with new synergies designed to reward players for accumulating and exhausting their daggers:Exhaust Interaction: Unlike the first game, core Shiv generators like Blade Dance now Exhaust, and Knife Trap cashes that in by replaying every Shiv sitting in your Exhaust Pile.Shiv Spread: Fan of Knives turns Shivs into AOE and refills your hand, while Phantom Blades adds Retain and a stronger single-hit payoff.Hidden Daggers: Hidden Daggers bridges both archetypes cleanly, discarding cards to trigger Sly while stocking your hand with more

metadata.title: The Silent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: while Phantom Blades adds Retain and a stronger single-hit payoff.Hidden Daggers: Hidden Daggers bridges both archetypes cleanly, discarding cards to trigger Sly while stocking your hand with more Shivs.Strategic ArchetypesThe Silent's toolkit in Slay the Spire 2 supports several powerful build paths, each enhanced by the new Sly keyword:🗡️ Sly Discard EngineWith Sly as the backbone, discard-focused decks turn discard from a cost into a payoff. Pair enablers like Dagger Throw with Reflex and Tactician to convert discard into both draw and energy.🔪 Shiv StormGenerate Shivs with Blade Dance, spread them across the board with Fan of Knives, then cash in your Exhaust Pile with Knife Trap.🛡️ The Dexterity TankAnticipate gives the Silent a temporary Dexterity spike, enabling much stronger defensive turns exactly when she needs them.🃏 Hand ManagementWell-Laid Plans holds the right cards for the right turn, while Hand Trick converts that stored Skill into a temporary Sly payoff so defense

metadata.title: The Silent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

When you select a level, all modifiers at that level and below apply.0No AscensionPlay without any Ascension modifiers.1Swarming ElitesElites spawn more often.2Weary TravelerAncients only heal 80% of your missing HP.3PovertyEnemies and Treasure Chests drop 25% less Gold.4Tight BeltStart each run with 1 less potion slot.5Ascender's BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Regent is a new playable

metadata.title: The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Regent is a new playable character in Slay the Spire 2, formally revealed in the December 2025 "Neowsletter." A sophisticated and mysterious figure, The Regent brings an entirely new resource management system to Slay the Spire 2's roguelike deckbuilding formula. Their playstyle focuses on resource accumulation and card transformation, drawing comparisons to the "scaling" nature of the Defect from the original Slay the Spire.Core Combat MechanicsThe Star SystemUnique Resource: A brand-new mechanic in Slay the Spire 2 — in addition to Energy, The Regent manages a secondary resource called Stars.Persistence: Unlike Energy, which resets every turn, Stars are accumulated and carried over between turns.Star Cost: Many powerful cards have a 0 Energy cost but require a Star cost to play. For example, Falling Star costs 0

metadata.title: The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: which resets every turn, Stars are accumulated and carried over between turns.Star Cost: Many powerful cards have a 0 Energy cost but require a Star cost to play. For example, Falling Star costs 0 Energy but requires 2 Stars.Strategy: Players must balance generating Stars early and cashing them into finishers later, with cards like Hidden Cache letting you bank part of that burst for the next turn.The Transform MechanicHand Manipulation: The Regent can temporarily transform cards in their hand into specific tactical tokens.Using cards like GUARDS!!! will transform your entire hand into Minion Sacrifice cards (which provide Block and Exhaust).Strategy: This acts as a defensive panic button, but alongside CHARGE!! and BEGONE! it also turns dead cards into fresh minion tools and feeds Supermassive with more created cards.The Forge MechanicScaling Weaponry: Similar to "Rampage," cards with the Forge keyword permanently increase the power of a specific card for the duration of the

metadata.title: The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: Supermassive with more created cards.The Forge MechanicScaling Weaponry: Similar to "Rampage," cards with the Forge keyword permanently increase the power of a specific card for the duration of the combat.The Sovereign Blade: The centerpiece of this mechanic. Cards like Summon Forth will "Forge" the Sovereign Blade (increasing its damage) and move it into your hand from the draw pile, discard pile, or exhaust pile.Retain: The forged weapon typically has the Retain keyword, allowing you to build up a massive "one-shot" kill while waiting for the perfect window.Synergies with Colorless CardsIn Slay the Spire 2, The Regent has a unique affinity for Colorless (Neutral) Cards, more so than any other character:Resource Generation: Spectrum Shift acts as an upgraded "Magnetism," adding a random Colorless card to your hand at the start of every turn.Scaling: Supermassive counts every card you created this combat, so it scales not only from Spectrum Shift but also from transformation tools

metadata.title: The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: Colorless card to your hand at the start of every turn.Scaling: Supermassive counts every card you created this combat, so it scales not only from Spectrum Shift but also from transformation tools like GUARDS!!!.Strategic OverviewCombat Tempo: Among Slay the Spire 2's roster, The Regent is a "slow-burn" character. They typically have a weak Turn 1, requiring setup to generate Stars and Forge their Blade.Difficulty: High. One of Slay the Spire 2's most demanding characters — success depends on meticulous resource tracking, knowing exactly when to save Stars and when to transform your hand.Key Archetypes⭐ Star BurstBank Stars with Hidden Cache, then unload that burst through 0-cost Star payoffs like Falling Star.🗡️ Sovereign ForgeFocusing entirely on scaling the Sovereign Blade into a massive damage-dealer via Summon Forth and other Forge cards.🌈 Colorless EvolutionUse Spectrum Shift to keep creating cards, then let Supermassive convert that growing created-card count into a scaling

metadata.title: The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: damage-dealer via Summon Forth and other Forge cards.🌈 Colorless EvolutionUse Spectrum Shift to keep creating cards, then let Supermassive convert that growing created-card count into a scaling neutral payoff.🪖 Minion ConversionUse GUARDS!!!, CHARGE!!, and BEGONE! to convert dead cards into useful minion tools while also increasing your created-card total.Explore MoreThe Regent Strategy GuideStars, Forge, Minions & build pathsNecrobinder — Doom, Souls & OstyIronclad — Strength, Exhaust & VulnerabilityThe Silent — Sly, Shivs & PoisonDetailsNo additional details availableComments (0)Be the first to comment.Sign in to join the discussionSign inSlaytheSpire2.ggSlay the Spire 2 database, tools, and community links.© 2026 SlaytheSpire2.ggPage NavigationCharactersCardsRelicsPotionsEnemiesEncountersEventsAncientsTimelineGuidesTier List MakerLegal & SitePrivacy PolicyTerms of UseAbout UsrequestAnimationFrame(function(){$RT=performance.now()});The Regent – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the

When you select a level, all modifiers at that level and below apply.0No AscensionPlay without any Ascension modifiers.1Swarming ElitesElites spawn more often.2Weary TravelerAncients only heal 80% of your missing HP.3PovertyEnemies and Treasure Chests drop 25% less Gold.4Tight BeltStart each run with 1 less potion slot.5Ascender's BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Necrobinder is a new playable character in Slay the Spire 2,

pageContent: are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Necrobinder is a new playable character in Slay the Spire 2, bringing a completely unique "proxy-fighting" style to the game. Unlike previous characters who stand alone against the horrors of the Spire, she relies on a symbiotic relationship with her colossal reanimated companion, Osty, and a terrifying lethal threshold mechanic known as Doom. She offers the most complex and rewarding tactical experience in Slay the Spire 2 to date.The Companion System: Osty & Proxy CombatOstyA colossal skeletal companion unique to Slay the Spire 2's Necrobinder. Acts as a dynamic Meat Shield, automatically absorbing incoming attack damage.Proxy Defense: Osty creates a "pseudo-block" layer independent of standard Block stacks, automatically absorbing damage that would hit the player.Summon & Revive: Pull Aggro is essential for summoning or reviving Osty if his HP falls to

metadata.title: Necrobinder – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: layer independent of standard Block stacks, automatically absorbing damage that would hit the player.Summon & Revive: Pull Aggro is essential for summoning or reviving Osty if his HP falls to zero, while Devour Life turns every Soul you play into steady Summon scaling.Visual Scaling: Osty actually grows in size as his HP increases, providing a clear visual indicator of your defensive strength.Weaponize: Cards like Bone Shards offer high AOE damage and Block at the cost of sacrificing Osty, requiring you to carefully time his resurrection.The Doom MechanicLethal Threshold: A signature mechanic new to Slay the Spire 2 — Doom is a debuff that tracks a lethal threshold. If a creature's total Doom stacks meet or exceed its current HP, it is instantly executed at the end of its turn.Stacking Doom: Use cards like Blight Strike, Deathbringer, and Scourge to rapidly accumulate Doom stacks.Finisher: The rare attack Time's Up! deals damage equal to the target's current Doom, often triggering an

metadata.title: Necrobinder – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: Use cards like Blight Strike, Deathbringer, and Scourge to rapidly accumulate Doom stacks.Finisher: The rare attack Time's Up! deals damage equal to the target's current Doom, often triggering an immediate execution.Double-Edged Sword: Neurosurge grants massive resources but applies Doom to yourself every turn, effectively putting the player on a countdown to their own demise.Soul Harvesting & Ethereal VelocitySoul Tokens: A Soul is a 0-cost token that draws 2 cards and exhausts, acting as the Necrobinder's primary engine for deck cycling in Slay the Spire 2.Generation: Use defensive cards like Grave Warden to seed Souls steadily, or let Severance blast Souls into your draw pile, hand, and discard pile at once to accelerate the engine immediately.Ethereal Synergy: Many of her strongest cards, such as Defile and Enfeebling Touch, vanish if not played. But with Pagestorm, every Ethereal card drawn triggers even more card draw, enabling explosive "velocity" turns where the Necrobinder

metadata.title: Necrobinder – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: cards, such as Defile and Enfeebling Touch, vanish if not played. But with Pagestorm, every Ethereal card drawn triggers even more card draw, enabling explosive "velocity" turns where the Necrobinder plays dozens of cards in a single cycle.Strategic OverviewPlaying the Necrobinder in Slay the Spire 2 requires a shift in mindset from traditional health management to resource-proxy management. She is one of STS2's most mechanically complex characters.Key Archetypes🛡️ Osty WallBuild an Osty wall with Devour Life and Pull Aggro, then let Grave Warden feed both defense and more Souls so the companion keeps scaling.💀 Doom RushStack Doom rapidly with Blight Strike, Deathbringer, and Scourge, then cash out with Time's Up!.👻 Soul VelocityUse Severance and Soul tokens to load multiple zones, then chain Ethereal cards like Defile and Enfeebling Touch through Pagestorm for explosive turns.⚡ Self-Doom BurstUse Neurosurge for massive energy generation at the cost of applying Doom to yourself —

metadata.title: Necrobinder – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: chain Ethereal cards like Defile and Enfeebling Touch through Pagestorm for explosive turns.⚡ Self-Doom BurstUse Neurosurge for massive energy generation at the cost of applying Doom to yourself — burst down bosses before the countdown runs out.Explore MoreNecrobinder Strategy GuideIn-depth mechanics, combos, and build pathsThe Regent — Stars, Forge & MinionsIronclad — Strength, Exhaust & VulnerabilityThe Silent — Sly, Shivs & PoisonDetailsNo additional details availableComments (0)Be the first to comment.Sign in to join the discussionSign inSlaytheSpire2.ggSlay the Spire 2 database, tools, and community links.© 2026 SlaytheSpire2.ggPage NavigationCharactersCardsRelicsPotionsEnemiesEncountersEventsAncientsTimelineGuidesTier List MakerLegal & SitePrivacy PolicyTerms of UseAbout UsrequestAnimationFrame(function(){$RT=performance.now()});Necrobinder – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2document.querySelectorAll('body link[rel="icon"], body

Channels elemental power to deal damage, gain Block, generate Energy, and set up explosive Evoke turns.EarlyAccessStartingHP75Gold99Max Energy3Orb Slots3Starting RelicCracked CoreUnlockComplete a single run with Necrobinder to unlock this character.Starting Deck×4Strike4 copies×4Defend4 copiesZapDualcastBrowse all The Defect cards →Browse The Defect relics →AscensionAll characters share the same Ascension track (levels 0–10). When you select a level, all modifiers at that level and below apply.0No AscensionPlay without any Ascension modifiers.1Swarming ElitesElites spawn more often.2Weary TravelerAncients only heal 80% of your missing HP.3PovertyEnemies and Treasure Chests drop 25% less Gold.4Tight BeltStart each run with 1 less potion slot.5Ascender's BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to

metadata.title: The Defect – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: BaneStart each run Cursed.6InflationRemoving cards from your deck at the Merchant is more expensive.7ScarcityRare and Upgraded cards appear less often.8Tough EnemiesAll enemies are harder to kill.9Deadly EnemiesAll enemies have deadlier attacks.10Double BossFight two bosses at the end of Act 3.BackgroundThe Defect returns in Slay the Spire 2 as the Orb-driven automaton, but its sequel toolkit is broader than just replaying the old formula. Lightning, Frost, Dark, and Plasma are all here, and the newly revealed Glass Orb gives the class a fresh AOE damage axis on top of its traditional scaling engine.Core Mechanics: Orbs, Focus & Slot ManagementChannel & Evoke: The Defect still revolves around Channel and Evoke. Channeling places an Orb into an empty slot; if all slots are full, the leftmost Orb is automatically Evoked to make room. Active Evoke cards like Dualcast and Multi-Cast instead consume your rightmost Orb on demand.Orb Suite: Lightning provides steady damage, Frost provides

metadata.title: The Defect – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: Orb is automatically Evoked to make room. Active Evoke cards like Dualcast and Multi-Cast instead consume your rightmost Orb on demand.Orb Suite: Lightning provides steady damage, Frost provides Block, Dark stores a growing burst that cashes out on Evoke, Plasma generates Energy, and the newly revealed Glass Orb deals AOE damage that decays each turn.Focus & Slots: Defragment, Biased Cognition, and Hotfix scale Orb output, while Capacitor and Modded expand your slot count so more Orbs can stay online at once.Unique-Orb Payoffs: The current Defect pool clearly rewards mixing Orb types instead of tunnel-visioning one element. Compile Driver, Coolant, and Synchronize all scale off the number of unique Orbs you have, with Rainbow acting as the cleanest starter.Strategic ArchetypesThe current Defect is no longer just a classic Orb shell. The pool supports mixed-Orb engines, Glass tempo, Power scaling, 0-cost recursion, and even Status-card recycling as real archetypes:🌈 Mixed Orb

metadata.title: The Defect – Cards, Strategy & Builds | Slay the Spire 2

pageContent: current Defect is no longer just a classic Orb shell. The pool supports mixed-Orb engines, Glass tempo, Power scaling, 0-cost recursion, and even Status-card recycling as real archetypes:🌈 Mixed Orb EngineBuild around Rainbow, Compile Driver, Coolant, and Synchronize to convert Orb diversity into draw, defense, and temporary Focus spikes.🪞 Glass TempoThe new Orb package of Glasswork, Refract, and Spinner lets the Defect build reliable AOE pressure, then cash everything out with Shatter.⚡ Power EngineUse Storm, Subroutine, Creative AI, and Signal Boost to turn every Power card into extra channeling, extra Energy, or compounding value.🧪 Status RecyclingThe current Defect also supports a surprisingly deep Status package. Compact transforms Status cards into Fuel, Iteration rewards drawing them, and Trash to Treasure, Smokestack, Flak Cannon, and Rocket Punch all turn junk into payoff.🦾 Zero-Cost RecursionThe classic low-cost Defect shell is still alive. Claw, All for One, Feral, and

pageContent: and TearAttackUncommonVersionInitialUpgradedSquashAttackEventVersionInitialUpgradedStackSkillEventVersionInitialUpgradedToric ToughnessSkillEventVersionInitialUpgradedAd slotSlay the Spire 2 Cards overviewSlay the Spire 2 cards are the core of your deckbuilder run. Every character — Ironclad, Silent, Defect, Necrobinder, and the Regent — has a unique card pool with its own mechanics, plus a shared set of Colorless and Quest cards that appear across multiple runs. This Slay the Spire 2 card database lets you browse every card by character, type, rarity, and energy cost.Use the filters and character tabs above to focus on the card pool you care about most. Whether you are planning an Ironclad Strength deck, a Silent discard-combo build, a Defect Orb engine, a Necrobinder Doom setup, or a Regent Stars and Forge shell, this Slay the Spire 2 card list is built to be quickly scannable while you theorycraft.Card types and rarities in Slay the Spire 2Slay the Spire 2 cards still follow the

metadata.title: All Slay the Spire 2 Cards: Database, Costs, Effects & Guide

pageContent: Regent Stars and Forge shell, this Slay the Spire 2 card list is built to be quickly scannable while you theorycraft.Card types and rarities in Slay the Spire 2Slay the Spire 2 cards still follow the classic Attack, Skill, and Power types, but each character also adds twists through mechanics like Sly, Doom, Stars, and Orbs. Cards come in Common, Uncommon, Rare, and special rarities such as Quest and Colorless. Rarity hints at power, but the strongest cards are those that fit your deck's plan and energy curve, not just the ones with gold borders.Building a consistent deck with this card listConsistency wins runs. When browsing this Slay the Spire 2 card database, pay attention to how many cheap cards, scaling pieces, defensive tools, and payoff cards you are taking. Aim for an energy curve that lets you reliably play your hand every turn, rather than stuffing your deck with expensive, situational cards that clog your draws.Slay the Spire 2 Cards FAQHow many cards should be in my

pageContent: curve that lets you reliably play your hand every turn, rather than stuffing your deck with expensive, situational cards that clog your draws.Slay the Spire 2 Cards FAQHow many cards should be in my deck?There is no perfect number, but thinner decks are usually more consistent. In Slay the Spire 2, try to remove low-impact starter cards and avoid adding mediocre choices just because they are offered. Use this card list to identify which Slay the Spire 2 cards are true upgrades for your current deck, and skip the rest.Should I prioritize upgrades or new cards?Upgrading key cards can be more valuable than adding new ones, especially for core payoff cards, scaling engines, and crucial defensive tools. Use this card database to compare base and upgraded versions of important Slay the Spire 2 cards, then decide whether your next campfire should be a Rest or a Smith.How do character mechanics change card evaluation?Each character's unique mechanics — Sly, Doom, Stars, Orbs, and more —

pageContent: cards, then decide whether your next campfire should be a Rest or a Smith.How do character mechanics change card evaluation?Each character's unique mechanics — Sly, Doom, Stars, Orbs, and more — dramatically shift how strong certain cards become. A card that looks weak in isolation can be incredible when combined with the right relics or supporting pieces. As you browse this Slay the Spire 2 card list, always evaluate cards in the context of your current synergies instead of using fixed tier lists.Planning routes and fights around your cardsUsing this card list to spot missing piecesBefore major decisions — shops, elites, or boss fights — use this Slay the Spire 2 card list to check which tools your deck is missing. If you lack scalable damage, look for cards that add Strength, multi-hit attacks, or repeatable sources of damage. If your defense is weak, prioritize reliable Block cards, scaling defensive Powers, or cards that thin your deck.Aligning pathing with your card planYour
